reasoning: Barcelona has successfully secured the first leg of the treble, winning the Copa del Rey final against Real Madrid (3-2) on April 26. The Treble now depends on La Liga and the Champions League. In La Liga, as of April 23, they have a 7-point lead over Real Madrid with just a few games remaining. While they still face an El Clasico matchup, a 7-point cushion provides a ~90-95% probability of winning the league. In the Champions League, they have advanced to the semi-finals to face Inter Milan, with a potential final against Arsenal or PSG. Bookmaker odds generally price them around 2/1 (33% implied, ~28-30% true probability) to win the UCL outright, while statistical models like Opta put them slightly lower (~22%). Assuming a ~30% chance for the UCL and ~92% for La Liga, their mathematical chance for the remaining treble is simply 0.92 * 0.30 ≈ 28%. The April 17 PM price of 0.21 (before the Copa final) is consistent with this, as PMs often lightly overprice parlays (true pre-Copa chance was likely ~15%). Removing the 50-55% hurdle of the Copa final naturally puts the updated probability in the 28-31% range.
